response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer with strong breadth across web, mobile, and some embedded/low-latency work, but the role is a Principal-level hardware performance architecture position centered on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator optimization, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows none of these core competencies — no AI/deep-learning workload experience, no SoC/IP modeling, no GPU or accelerator architecture work, and no relevant advanced degree. The closest adjacencies (C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, robotic-arm work) are tangential and dated relative to what a Principal IC in this domain requires.
strengths: Deep, long-tenured software engineering experience across many domains
Some low-latency/high-performance backend work (DLang async I/O, high-throughput services)
Prior C/C++ exposure and embedded/hardware-adjacent projects
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU architecture experience
Educational path and seniority profile do not match a Principal hardware architect IC

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No deep-learning workload or GPU architecture experience
